title: [Bug]:  Can't initialize iptables table NAT on MacOS
body:
### Issue Description

If I run `iptables --list -t nat` command with `docker` it works just fine, however it breaks with `podman`. 
What I am missing?


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run`podman run -it --rm --cap-add=NET_ADMIN --cap-add=NET_RAW alpine`

**Run Commands in Container**
```shell
apk add iptables
iptables --list -t nat
```

### Describe the results you received

```
iptables v1.8.8 (legacy): can't initialize iptables table `nat': Permission denied
Perhaps iptables or your kernel needs to be upgraded.
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
Chain PREROUTING (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain POSTROUTING (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Additional information

I am the author of https://github.com/qoomon/docker-host and want to support podman as well.

comments.body: It does work for me (output is as expected), even though your setup is practically identical to mine, with 2 exceptions. I am using ext4 instead of xfs (highly unlikely that's the culprit though), and you are using SELinux, I am not. However, I remember now running into this issue a long time ago as well. It may seem silly, but have you rebooted your machine? I seem to remember that that's what fixed my problem after some googling around, due to some kernel module (it might have been `iptable_nat`) not being loaded or something, and `insmod iptable_nat` not making the error go away.

Yes this look like the kernel module is not loaded. As root iptables will just load the module for you. However since you run podman rootless that is impossible.
The easy fix is to load the modules as root before you execute podman, alternatively you can run podman as root.
see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/modules-load.d/podman-iptables.conf

Or you use iptables-nft this doesn't require root to load the modules AFAIK.

I am closing this because it is not a bug but feel free to continue the discussion.
I just realized you are using podman machine, can you check if `/usr/lib/modules-load.d/podman-iptables.conf` is present in the VM? You can access the VM with `podman machine ssh`.

@Luap99 yes it is present with following content
```
# On fedora 36 ip_tables is no longer auto loaded and rootless user have no permsissions to load it.
# When we have actual nftables support in the future we might want to revisit this.
# If you use iptables-nft this is not needed.
ip_tables
ip6_tables
```

Strange It works on my machine as well after I added `nf_nat`kernel module to podman machine
```
podman machine ssh

modprobe nf_nat
```
This strange because I am only using `iptables` commands see https://github.com/qoomon/docker-host/blob/master/entrypoint.sh

Strange, but maybe not unexpected if you check out [this page](https://www.kernelconfig.io/config_ip_nf_nat):

> iptables NAT support
modulename: iptable_nat.ko
configname: CONFIG_IP_NF_NAT
Linux Kernel Configuration
└─> Networking support
└─> Networking options
└─> Network packet filtering framework (Netfilter)
└─> IP: Netfilter Configuration
└─> iptables NAT support
> 
> This enables the `nat' table in iptables. This allows masquerading,
port forwarding and other forms of full Network Address Port
Translation. 

But glad that it now works for you! :)
